pi_items.source: Path verified in setup_task.sh and task.json
Path verified in task.json and export_result.sh
Task description specifies 20 meter contour interval
Task description specifies index contours at multiples of 100m

SRTM file srtm_41_19.tif downloaded from https://github.com/qgis/QGIS-Training-Data/raw/master/exercise_data/raster/SRTM/srtm_41_19.tif and analyzed with tifffile. Actual minimum elevation value is 4m (non-zero data; 0 values are nodata/boundary pixels).
SRTM file srtm_41_19.tif downloaded and analyzed. Actual maximum elevation value is 1699m.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real SRTM elevation data from the official QGIS training repository (srtm_41_19.tif, 1.1MB). The data is a digital elevation model with 661x837 pixels covering a mountainous region. However, the elevation range in the metadata is incorrect: the actual data contains elevations from 4m to 1699m, not the claimed 800m to 1800m. With a 20m contour interval, the raster would generate approximately 86 contour lines, of which 18 would be index contours (at 100m multiples: 0, 100, 200, ..., 1700m). The task paths and interval values are correctly specified.
